title: butea littoralis
intro: Butea littoralis, also known as the Siris tree, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Butea littoralis can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
